market_overview.headline: Market in complete bear structure with SPY, QQQ, and BTC all in Full Bear signals amid escalating Iran conflict.
market_overview.detail: Only 23% of stocks in Full Bull vs 40% in Full Bear, with all major indices showing crossover alerts suggesting potential bottoming attempts. Energy and industrial services remain the only bright spots while tech and finance are getting crushed.

items.name: Netflix, Inc.
items.note: Large-cap showing relative strength above 13W with tight crossover setup brewing

items.name: NVIDIA Corporation
items.note: AI king showing potential long-term EMA reversal setup despite Full Bear structure

items.name: Apple Inc.
items.note: Mega-cap tech with tight 13W/21W convergence suggesting potential trend shift

items.name: JP Morgan Chase & Co.
items.note: Banking giant showing early signs of EMA structure repair after brutal selloff

items.name: Vertiv Holdings, LLC
items.note: Data center play with low volume pullback suggests orderly selling and higher conviction entry

items.name: FedEx Corporation
items.note: Logistics giant showing controlled pullback on light volume ideal for swing entries

items.name: Sandisk Corporation
items.note: Storage play still well above 21W EMA with healthy pullback pattern intact

items.name: Coca-Cola Company (The)
items.note: Defensive consumer staple offering safe haven in volatile market environment

momentum_themes.headline: Energy Dominance Continues
momentum_themes.detail: Energy minerals sector showing 100% bull participation with names like OXY, CVE, VLO leading the charge. Industrial services also strong with infrastructure plays benefiting from ongoing capex themes.

sector_analysis.headline: Energy vs Tech Divergence Extreme

strongest.note: Perfect bull participation as Iran war premium drives oil complex
Small sector showing resilient performance in defensive rotation
Infrastructure and utility services benefiting from war-driven capex needs

weakest.note: Brutal selloff with mega-caps like MSFT, META, GOOGL all deeply oversold
Auto sector crushed with TSLA, GM, F all in severe downtrends
Business services seeing complete breakdown as recession fears mount

sector_analysis.detail: Classic flight-to-safety rotation with energy/utilities outperforming while growth tech gets decimated. Finance sector particularly vulnerable with 84% bear participation as rate fears persist.

items.text: All major indices in Full Bear structure with SPY -7.8% YTD — broad market breakdown confirmed
Technology services showing 91% bear participation — avoid growth names until trend reversal confirmed
BTC -49% below fair value at $66K signals extreme risk-off environment — crypto correlation risk high
Iran war escalation creating unpredictable geopolitical premium in energy while crushing risk assets

news_drivers.headline: What Moved Markets
news_drivers.summary: Iranian missile attacks on Kuwait infrastructure and expanding Middle East conflict dominated headlines, driving oil futures toward record monthly gains. Geopolitical uncertainty crushed risk assets while energy sector saw massive inflows as war premium expanded.

items.text: Iranian attacks on Kuwait power plants killed workers, escalating regional conflict fears
Brent crude heading for record monthly gains as Strait of Hormuz tensions spike
G7 pledging stability measures while Netanyahu orders expanded Lebanon operations
Gulf markets selling off on broader Iran war concerns despite infrastructure investments
Trump administration uncertainty about Iran war endgame creating additional market volatility
